symptoms.symptom_name: Acute bilateral masseter/temporalis spasm with trismus and referred pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD myofascial spasm with central sensitisation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral rock-hard masseter/temporalis spasm with trismus after bruxism is myofascial TMD.
Bilateral muscular spasm without focal swelling is against a localized dental abscess.

symptoms.symptom_name: No submandibular/neck swelling, drooling, or breathing/saliva-swallowing difficulty

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of neck/floor-of-mouth swelling, drooling and airway compromise argues strongly against a deep-space infection threatening the airway.

symptoms.symptom_name: No scalp tenderness/jaw claudication/visual change (GCA screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No scalp tenderness/jaw claudication/visual change and a constant spasm (not chewing-induced claudication) argue against GCA, which is also uncommon at this age.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal dental abscess features/fever; tetanus up to date, no descending rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No focal swelling/pus/fever argues against a dental abscess.
Up-to-date vaccination and no generalized descending rigidity/risus sardonicus argue against tetanus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Jaw hard to open (trismus) but not fixed dislocated open; reduced intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A jaw stuck closed from spasm (not fixed open) is against an acute open dislocation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rock-hard tender masseter/temporalis, ~15mm opening, non-tender temporal arteries, trapezius spasm (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD myofascial spasm with central sensitisation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral masseter spasm with reduced opening and non-tender temporal arteries fits myofascial TMD.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD myofascial spasm with central sensitisation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central sensitisation amplifies TMD pain and the systemic ache.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: TMD myofascial spasm with central sensitisation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute bilateral masseter spasm with trismus and referred temporal/neck pain after bruxism, with no airway/deep-space-infection features, no GCA features and no fixed dislocation, is myofascial TMD — but the jaw/mouth can't be examined remotely and trismus limits intake, so urgent in-person (dental/GP) review plus supportive care and hydration are required, with emergency care for airway swelling, GCA features or a jaw stuck open.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person dental/GP examination (masseter/TMJ, intra-oral, jaw opening)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess the TMJ/muscles, exclude a dental/deep-space infection, and plan management; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ESR/CRP + temporal artery assessment ONLY if GCA features
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated without cranial ischaemic features in a young patient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Dental imaging (OPG) if a dental cause is suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: If the exam suggests an odontogenic source.
